headline: Chip stocks surge after-hours on Micron’s record quarter
summary: Investing.com - Micron Technology Inc (NASDAQ:MU) delivered the strongest quarterly results in its history after Wednesday's close, posting fiscal third-quarter 2026 revenue of $41.46 billion against a Wall Street consensus of $35.69 billion, a 16.2% beat, and earnings per share of $25.11 versus the $20.49 estimate, a 22.6% upside surprise. The print sent Micron shares up roughly 12.6% in extended trade to around $1,180.97, recovering from a regular-session close of $1,047.20.

raw.headline: Chip stocks surge after-hours on Micron’s record quarter

The print sent Micron shares up roughly 12.6% in extended trade to around $1,180.97, recovering from a regular-session close of $1,047.20.

headline: Qualcomm bets on AI chips to break smartphone reliance, but faces crowded race
summary: Qualcomm is expected to use its investor day on Wednesday to lay out a push beyond its core smartphone business into ‌the fast-growing, but highly competitive, market for AI data center chips.  Analysts ‌expect the San Diego-based company to name new customers for its AI chips as it tries to ​gain a foothold in a market dominated by Nvidia.  The shift reflects mounting pressure in the smartphone market, where Qualcomm is one of the world’s largest chip suppliers to Android device makers.

headline: Qualcomm to buy AI startup Modular for $4 billion in AI software push
summary: Qualcomm said on Wednesday it would buy AI startup Modular in an all-stock deal valued at nearly $4 billion, gaining ‌access to software that runs AI models across chips without having to ‌write code for each processor.  Buying Modular pits Qualcomm against CUDA, the software platform that has helped ​underpin Nvidia's AI dominance by tying millions of developers to the $5 trillion company's chips.  As part of the deal, Qualcomm expects to issue up to 19.2 million shares of its common stock to Modular's equity holders.

raw.headline: Qualcomm to buy AI startup Modular for $4 billion in AI software push
raw.summary: Qualcomm said on Wednesday it would buy AI startup Modular in an all-stock deal valued at nearly $4 billion, gaining ‌access to software that runs AI models across chips without having to ‌write code for each processor.  Buying Modular pits Qualcomm against CUDA, the software platform that has helped ​underpin Nvidia's AI dominance by tying millions of developers to the $5 trillion company's chips.  As part of the deal, Qualcomm expects to issue up to 19.2 million shares of its common stock to Modular's equity holders.

headline: Qualcomm in talks to provide custom chip-design services to ByteDance, sources say
summary: Qualcomm is in talks to provide chip-design services to China's ByteDance, four people familiar with the matter said, as the U.S. company seeks ‌to reduce dependence on the smartphone market, its biggest revenue source.  If successful, the ‌negotiations would make ByteDance, the parent of short-video platform TikTok, an early customer of Qualcomm's chip-design services operation.  ​Qualcomm is the world's largest supplier of smartphone modem chips, which manage cellular communications.

raw.headline: Qualcomm in talks to provide custom chip-design services to ByteDance, sources say
raw.summary: Qualcomm is in talks to provide chip-design services to China's ByteDance, four people familiar with the matter said, as the U.S. company seeks ‌to reduce dependence on the smartphone market, its biggest revenue source.  If successful, the ‌negotiations would make ByteDance, the parent of short-video platform TikTok, an early customer of Qualcomm's chip-design services operation.  ​Qualcomm is the world's largest supplier of smartphone modem chips, which manage cellular communications.

headline: Qualcomm Eyes TikTok Parent ByteDance For Custom Chip Design Services: Report
summary: Qualcomm is reportedly in talks with ByteDance to design custom AI chips and VPUs, with mass production eyed by year-end.

raw.headline: Qualcomm Eyes TikTok Parent ByteDance For Custom Chip Design Services: Report
raw.summary: Qualcomm is reportedly in talks with ByteDance to design custom AI chips and VPUs, with mass production eyed by year-end.

headline: Qualcomm (QCOM) Is In Talks To Buy Modular And Push Further Into AI Chips
summary: Qualcomm (NasdaqGS:QCOM) is reported to be in advanced talks to acquire AI chip startup Modular Inc. in a multi billion dollar deal. The potential transaction would mark a push by Qualcomm to build out its AI infrastructure and data center offerings beyond its core smartphone chip business. The negotiations are ongoing and the deal, if completed, would represent a major move into markets currently led by larger AI chip competitors. For investors watching Qualcomm, this prospective...

headline: CNBC Daily Open: Trump takes on big oil
summary: President Trump accuses energy companies of price "gouging" customers by keeping oil prices at the pump higher.
